findings.title: bitsandbytes-foundation/bitsandbytes
findings.description: Accessible large language models via k-bit quantization for PyTorch.

findings.title: Lightning-AI/lit-llama
findings.description: Implementation of the LLaMA language model based on nanoGPT. Supports flash attention, Int8 and GPTQ 4bit quantization, LoRA and LLaMA-Adapter fine-tuning, pre-training. Apache 2.0-licensed.

findings.description: GGUF Quantization support for native ComfyUI models

findings.title: thu-ml/SageAttention
findings.description: [ICLR2025, ICML2025, NeurIPS2025 Spotlight] Quantized Attention achieves speedup of 2-5x compared to FlashAttention, without losing end-to-end metrics across language, image, and video models.

findings.title: huawei-noah/Pretrained-Language-Model
findings.description: Pretrained language model and its related optimization techniques developed by Huawei Noah's Ark Lab.

findings.title: IntelLabs/nlp-architect
findings.description: A model library for exploring state-of-the-art deep learning topologies and techniques for optimizing Natural Language Processing neural networks

findings.description: Base pretrained models and datasets in pytorch (MNIST, SVHN, CIFAR10, CIFAR100, STL10, AlexNet, VGG16, VGG19, ResNet, Inception, SqueezeNet)

findings.title: intel/neural-compressor
findings.description: SOTA low-bit LLM quantization (INT8/FP8/MXFP8/INT4/MXFP4/NVFP4) & sparsity; leading model compression techniques on PyTorch, TensorFlow, and ONNX Runtime

findings.title: quic/aimet
findings.description: AIMET is a library that provides advanced quantization and compression techniques for trained neural network models.

findings.title: NVIDIA/Model-Optimizer
findings.description: A unified library of SOTA model optimization techniques like quantization, pruning, distillation, speculative decoding, etc. It compresses deep learning models for downstream deployment frameworks like TensorRT-LLM, TensorRT, vLLM, etc. to optimize inference speed.

findings.title: Efficient-ML/Awesome-Model-Quantization
findings.description: A list of papers, docs, codes about model quantization. This repo is aimed to provide the info for model quantization research, we are continuously improving the project. Welcome to PR the works (papers, repositories) that are missed by the repo.

findings.title: dvmazur/mixtral-offloading
findings.description: Run Mixtral-8x7B models in Colab or consumer desktops

findings.title: microsoft/Olive
findings.description: Olive: Simplify ML Model Finetuning, Conversion, Quantization, and Optimization for CPUs, GPUs and NPUs.

findings.title: 666DZY666/micronet
findings.description: micronet, a model compression and deploy lib. compression: 1、quantization: quantization-aware-training(QAT), High-Bit(>2b)(DoReFa/Quantization and Training of Neural Networks for Efficient Integer-Arithmetic-Only Inference)、Low-Bit(≤2b)/Ternary and Binary(TWN/BNN/XNOR-Net); post-training-quantization(PTQ), 8-bit(tensorrt); 2、 pruning: normal、regular and group convolutional channel pruning; 3、 group convolution structure; 4、batch-normalization fuse for quantization. deploy: tensorrt, fp32/fp16/int8(ptq-calibration)、op-adapt(upsample)、dynamic_shape

findings.description: OpenMMLab Model Compression Toolbox and Benchmark.

findings.description: PaddleSlim is an open-source library for deep model compression and architecture search.

findings.title: tensorflow/model-optimization
findings.description: A toolkit to optimize ML models for deployment for Keras and TensorFlow, including quantization and pruning.

findings.title: Repurposing 3D Generative Model for Autoregressive Layout Generation
findings.abstract: We introduce LaviGen, a framework that repurposes 3D generative models for 3D layout generation. findings.title: FineCog-Nav: Integrating Fine-grained Cognitive Modules for Zero-shot Multimodal UAV Navigation
findings.abstract: UAV vision-language navigation (VLN) requires an agent to navigate complex 3D environments from an egocentric perspective while following ambiguous multi-step instructions over long horizons. findings.title: Resolution-Agnostic Lensless Imaging via Fourier Neural Operators
findings.abstract: Lensless cameras based on thin diffusers offer a compact alternative to conventional refractive imaging but rely on computational reconstruction, since the diffuser's point spread function (PSF) globally multiplexes every scene point across the sensor. findings.title: Fluctuating Pair Density Wave in Finite-temperature Phase Diagram of the $t$-$t^\prime$ Hubbard Model
findings.abstract: The Hubbard model and its extensions are canonical theoretical frameworks for understanding correlated electronic states, including those in high-$T_c$ cuprates. findings.title: Global dynamics and regime shifts in a resource-consumer model with facilitation and habitat loss
findings.abstract: Modelling how populations respond to habitat loss is crucial for understanding ecosystem stability, especially when positive interactions among resource species, such as plant-plant facilitation, play a key role. Habitat loss not only reduces available organic nutrients and space for primary produce

findings.title: Phase transitions in Doi-Onsager, Noisy Transformer, and other multimodal models
findings.abstract: We study phase transitions for repulsive-attractive mean-field free energies on the circle. For a $\frac{1}{n+1}$-periodic interaction whose Fourier coefficients satisfy a certain decay condition, we prove that the critical coupling strength $K_c$ coincides with the linear stability threshold $K_\#$

findings.title: NaijaS2ST: A Multi-Accent Benchmark for Speech-to-Speech Translation in Low-Resource Nigerian Languages
findings.abstract: Speech translation for low-resource languages remains fundamentally limited by the scarcity of high-quality, diverse parallel speech data, a challenge that is especially pronounced in African linguistic contexts. findings.title: Recent progress on inflation and dark energy from string theory
findings.abstract: We review recent progress in string model building in both early and late time cosmology. findings.title: Using Large Language Models and Knowledge Graphs to Improve the Interpretability of Machine Learning Models in Manufacturing
findings.abstract: Explaining Machine Learning (ML) results in a transparent and user-friendly manner remains a challenging task of Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI). findings.title: Evaluating the Progression of Large Language Model Capabilities for Small-Molecule Drug Design
findings.abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have the potential to accelerate small molecule drug design due to their ability to reason about information from diverse sources and formats. However, their practical utility remains unclear due to the lack of benchmarks that reflect real-world scenarios. In this work,

findings.title: Learning to Reason with Insight for Informal Theorem Proving
findings.abstract: Although most of the automated theorem-proving approaches depend on formal proof systems, informal theorem proving can align better with large language models' (LLMs) strength in natural language processing. findings.title: No Universal Courtesy: A Cross-Linguistic, Multi-Model Study of Politeness Effects on LLMs Using the PLUM Corpus
findings.abstract: This paper explores the response of Large Language Models (LLMs) to user prompts with different degrees of politeness and impoliteness. findings.title: VEFX-Bench: A Holistic Benchmark for Generic Video Editing and Visual Effects
findings.abstract: As AI-assisted video creation becomes increasingly practical, instruction-guided video editing has become essential for refining generated or captured footage to meet professional requirements. Yet the field still lacks both a large-scale human-annotated dataset with complete editing examples and a

findings.title: From Benchmarking to Reasoning: A Dual-Aspect, Large-Scale Evaluation of LLMs on Vietnamese Legal Text
findings.abstract: The complexity of Vietnam's legal texts presents a significant barrier to public access to justice. findings.title: Hero-Mamba: Mamba-based Dual Domain Learning for Underwater Image Enhancement
findings.abstract: Underwater images often suffer from severe degradation, such as color distortion, low contrast, and blurred details, due to light absorption and scattering in water. While learning-based methods like CNNs and Transformers have shown promise, they face critical limitations: CNNs struggle to model the

findings.title: FL-MHSM: Spatially-adaptive Fusion and Ensemble Learning for Flood-Landslide Multi-Hazard Susceptibility Mapping at Regional Scale
findings.abstract: Existing multi-hazard susceptibility mapping (MHSM) studies often rely on spatially uniform models, treat hazards independently, and provide limited representation of cross-hazard dependence and uncertainty. To address these limitations, this study proposes a deep learning (DL) workflow for joint fl

findings.title: Information Router for Mitigating Modality Dominance in Vision-Language Models
findings.abstract: Vision Language models (VLMs) have demonstrated strong performance across a wide range of benchmarks, yet they often suffer from modality dominance, where predictions rely disproportionately on a single modality. findings.title: Semantic Area Graph Reasoning for Multi-Robot Language-Guided Search
findings.abstract: Coordinating multi-robot systems (MRS) to search in unknown environments is particularly challenging for tasks that require semantic reasoning beyond geometric exploration. Classical coordination strategies rely on frontier coverage or information gain and cannot incorporate high-level task intent,

findings.title: Atomistic Mechanisms of Stress-Dependent Molten Salt Corrosion in NiCr Alloys
findings.abstract: Ni-based structural alloys in molten salt environments often experience simultaneous mechanical loading and corrosive attack, yet the mechanisms governing stress-corrosion interactions remain unclear. findings.title: SwanNLP at SemEval-2026 Task 5: An LLM-based Framework for Plausibility Scoring in Narrative Word Sense Disambiguation
findings.abstract: Recent advances in language models have substantially improved Natural Language Understanding (NLU). Although widely used benchmarks suggest that Large Language Models (LLMs) can effectively disambiguate, their practical applicability in real-world narrative contexts remains underexplored.
